name: Catatumbo Lightning
primary_sources: Historical accounts by Alexander von Humboldt and Agustin Codazzi

visual_attributes.morphology: continuous lightning flashes within storm clouds
distinctive_features: visible for miles around Lake Maracaibo
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: originates from storm clouds at altitudes over 1 km

domain_specific_visual.occurrence_pattern: 140 to 160 nights a year, 9 hours per day

domain_specific_functional.ozone_production: produces a great quantity of ozone
domain_specific_functional.climate_interaction: influenced by ENSO and local atmospheric conditions

relationships.description: Occurs specifically over and around Lake Maracaibo in Venezuela.
Its variability is related to the ITCZ and other climate modes.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: specific to the region around Lake Maracaibo
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of strength and respect in Wayuu culture

contrastive_constraints: NOT unrelated to cultural or historical context of Venezuela
